records.ety: [F. dorsal, LL. dorsalis, fr. L. dorsualis, fr. dorsum back; cf. Gr. <?/, <?/, mountain ridge. Cf. Dorse, Dorsel, Dosel.]

defs: Pertaining to, or situated near, the back, or dorsum, of an animal or of one of its parts; notal; tergal; neural; as, the dorsal fin of a fish; the dorsal artery of the tongue; -- opposed to ventral.
Pertaining to the surface naturally inferior, as of a leaf.
Pertaining to the surface naturally superior, as of a creeping hepatic moss.

records.ety: [LL. dorsale, neut. fr. dorsalis. See Dorsal, a.]

defs: A hanging, usually of rich stuff, at the back of a throne, or of an altar, or in any similar position.
